what_it_does: This is a systemd timer unit that triggers company-agent-sessions-exporter.service every 20 minutes (OnCalendar=*:00/20), with Persistent=true, and is installed as a managed member of company.target rather than timers.target.
observations: Timer fires every 20 minutes on the hour and 20/40 past.
Persistent=true so missed triggers run on catch-up.
WantedBy=company.target binds the timer to the Company target.
Comments explain a 15-minute exporter quiesce window and a cadence tied to Session Fabric synthesis Round 1b.
Comments state the timer itself (not the oneshot service) is the managed member, matching ops/services.json.
standouts: Bound to company.target so it rises/falls with the Company stack and appears as a managed member.
Persistent=true combined with a cadence rationale tied to a 15-minute exporter quiesce window.
The timer unit, not the oneshot service, is declared as the managed member.
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit file sections [Unit], [Timer], [Install].
Uses WantedBy for install target membership.
Provides a descriptive Description and inline rationale comments.
notes: A persistent 20-minute systemd timer wired into company.target to keep the agent-sessions exporter visible as a managed Company unit.
questions: What exactly is Session Fabric synthesis Round 1b and how does it set the 20-minute cadence?
What does company-agent-sessions-exporter.service do when triggered?
What does ops/services.json declare about this job's managed unit?
What units are members of company.target and how is the target started/stopped?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a 20-minute persistent timer for the exporter, bound to company.target.
apparent_themes: Managed lifecycle and explicit target membership
Cadence alignment with downstream quiesce windows
intention_signals: It is trying to make the agent-sessions exporter run on a reliable, recoverable schedule while remaining visible and manageable as part of the Company target, rather than as an orphaned timer.
novelty: The timer unit itself is treated as the managed member of company.target, not the oneshot service it triggers, and the cadence is explicitly justified by a 15-minute exporter quiesce window and a named synthesis round.
contribution: Provides a persistent, recoverable 20-minute trigger for the agent-sessions exporter and an explicit company.target membership that keeps the timer visible in the managed Company lifecycle.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd timer unit that triggers company-agent-sessions-exporter.service every 20 minutes with Persistent=true and installs into company.target as a managed member.
intention_for_embedding: To schedule the agent-sessions exporter on a reliable cadence aligned with its quiesce window while keeping the timer visible within the Company target.
